_meta.experiment: #53 Paper G reinforcement — SAE feature absorption on the Spectra TWINS (TriLM ternary vs FloatLM FP), removing the BitNet-vs-Gemma model-identity confound

models.note: true twins: same LLaMA arch (30 layers, d=2304, vocab 50304), same data, same 300B tokens; differ only in precision

_meta.method: BatchTopK SAE (d_sae=6.4x d_in, k=32, 4000 steps, 400k acts) per layer; SAEBench feature-absorption over HookedTransformer-shim over HF; layers L6/15/23 = early/mid/late (same as BitNet)

verdict: POSITIVE: ternary (TriLM) absorption is NON-MONOTONIC in depth (V-shape 0.320->0.120->0.247: dips at mid, rises at late); the FP TWIN (FloatLM) is MONOTONE DECREASING (0.294->0.242->0.127). Mid: ternary ~2x LESS (0.120 vs 0.242, replicates Paper G '3x less at mid'). Late: ternary ~2x MORE (0.247 vs 0.127, replicates Paper G 'worse late'). This REPLICATES the Paper G depth non-monotonicity in a TRUE TWIN PAIR (removes the BitNet-vs-Gemma model-identity confound, the decisive blocker) AND in a SECOND ternary backbone (TriLM). Caveats: 1 twin pair, 1 seed, 1 layer/depth, untuned BatchTopK SAEs (abs absorption above published GemmaScope), TriLM late less extreme than BitNet (0.247 vs 0.648).
